paper_metadata.title: OPT: Open Pre-trained Transformer Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: Large language models, which are often trained for hundreds of thousands of compute days, have shown remarkable capabilities for zero- and few-shot learning. Given their computational cost, these models are difficult to replicate without significant capital. For the few that are available through APIs, no access is granted to the full model weights, making them difficult to study. We present Open Pre-trained Transformers (OPT), a suite of decoder-only pre-trained transformers ranging from 125M to 175B parameters, which we aim to fully and responsibly share with interested researchers. We show that OPT-175B is comparable to GPT-3, while requiring only 1/7th the carbon footprint to develop. We are also releasing our logbook detailing the infrastructure challenges we faced, along with code for experimenting with all of the released models.

extraction_data.innovation: OPT introduces a suite of open, pre-trained decoder-only Transformers trained on a large-scale, diverse corpus to enable reproducible and responsible research. The models are designed to match the performance of GPT-3 while reducing carbon footprint by 7x. OPT-175B is comparable to GPT-3 but requires only 1/7th the carbon footprint to develop.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Concatenation of RoBERTa corpus (BookCorpus, Stories, CC-News), The Pile (CommonCrawl, DM Mathematics, Project Gutenberg, HackerNews, OpenSubtitles, OpenWebText2, USPTO), and PushShift.io Reddit
extraction_data.extension: Uses a linear learning rate schedule with warmup and decay, and gradient clipping at 1.0 (reduced to 0.3 during mid-flight changes). Trained on 99,280 GB A100 GPUs, reaching 147 TFLOP/s per GPU utilization.
extraction_data.research_problem: Reproducible and responsible large language model research
extraction_data.application: Text generation, zero-shot and few-shot learning, language understanding, downstream task fine-tuning
extraction_data.paper_title: OPT: Open Pre-trained Transformer Language Models
